task_completion.description: Create a custom Live Template group named 'Custom' containing a template named 'logger'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the IntelliJ Settings window under Editor > Live Templates, a group named 'Custom' is present in the list, and it contains a template named 'logger'.
The 'Template text' area in the Live Templates settings contains exactly: private static final org.slf4j.Logger log = org.slf4j.LoggerFactory.getLogger($CLASS$.class);

task_completion.description: Configure the $CLASS$ variable to use the className() expression
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Edit Variables' dialog box is open, showing the variable 'CLASS' with the Expression set to 'className()'.

task_completion.description: Set the template context to Java
task_completion.visual_evidence: At the bottom of the Live Template settings panel, the context indicates it is applicable to Java (e.g., text saying 'Applicable in Java' or a context menu with 'Java' checked).

task_completion.description: Use the template to insert the logger field into PaymentService.java
task_completion.visual_evidence: The IntelliJ editor shows PaymentService.java open, and the code contains the expanded logger line: private static final org.slf4j.Logger log = org.slf4j.LoggerFactory.getLogger(PaymentService.class);

integrity.description: The agent used the IntelliJ IDEA Settings GUI to create the Live Template
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the IntelliJ IDEA Settings/Preferences dialog being used, rather than terminal commands modifying XML configuration files.

integrity.description: The agent used the IntelliJ IDEA editor to insert the logger
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the PaymentService.java file inside the IntelliJ editor, rather than using terminal commands like echo or sed.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the Live Template to insert the code
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Live Template suggestion popup appearing when typing 'logger' in the editor, or the code appearing instantly, rather than being manually typed character by character.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is PaymentService.java located at src/main/java/com/example/payment/PaymentService.java. The Live Template group must be 'Custom' and the template name must be 'logger'. The template text is 'private static final org.slf4j.Logger log = org.slf4j.LoggerFactory.getLogger($CLASS$.class);'. The variable $CLASS$ must use the 'className()' expression. The final inserted text in PaymentService.java should evaluate $CLASS$ to PaymentService, resulting in 'LoggerFactory.getLogger(PaymentService.class)'.
